step_by_step_thinking:
Given the patient is HBsAg positive but Anti- HBc Ab is negative, we suspect a scenario where the patient might have recently cleared the infection, possibly entering into the "window period" where the HBsAg has disappeared but Anti-HBs hasn't developed yet. During this window period, other markers like HBV DNA would help determine whether there is ongoing viral activity despite the lack of visible antigens. Checking HBV DNA now would allow us to assess if the virus is actively replicating, guiding appropriate follow-up actions.
\boxed{B} Based on the evidence indicating a possibility of recent clearance of HBV with entry into the 'window period', assessing HBV DNA helps clarify if there is ongoing viral replication without relying solely on currently indeterminate serologies.
